candidate_problem_record.subject: Chemistry
candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: Two batteries are constructed via the diagram below. The concentration of the solutions and the mass of the electrodes are identified in the data below the diagram. How would the voltage and the battery life for the two batteries compare?
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集更可能以单解题为主，不强推多解 agent，只保留基础可验证性与可标注性检查。

metadata.question_zh:
通过下图构建了两个电池。溶液的浓度和电极的质量在图下的数据中给出。比较两个电池的电压和电池寿命。

\begin{tabular}{c|c|c}
  & 电池 1 & 电池 2 \\
  \hline
  阳极 & $\mathrm{Zn}$ ($m = 10\ \mathrm{g}$) & $\mathrm{Zn}$ ($m = 20\ \mathrm{g}$) \\
  阴极 & $\mathrm{Cu}$ ($m = 5\ \mathrm{g}$) & $\mathrm{Cu}$ ($m = 10\ \mathrm{g}$) \\
  电解液 & $1\ \mathrm{mol/L}\ \mathrm{ZnSO_4}$ & $2\ \mathrm{mol/L}\ \mathrm{ZnSO_4}$
\end{tabular}

A. 电池 1 的电压小于电池 2，电池 1 的寿命小于电池 2
B. 电池 1 的电压小于电池 2，电池 1 的寿命等于电池 2
C. 电池 1 的电压等于电池 2，电池 1 的寿命等于电池 2
D. 电池 1 的电压等于电池 2，电池 1 的寿命小于电池 2
metadata.explanation_zh:
电池(galvanic cell)的电压由所涉及的半反应的还原电位决定,与标准还原电位和反应商($Q$)通过Nernst方程相关:

$E = E^\circ - \frac{RT}{nF} \ln Q$

其中$E^\circ$是标准还原电位,$R$是气体常数,$T$是绝对温度,$n$是转移的电子数,$F$是法拉第常数,$Q$是反应商。

对于给定的电池,反应商$Q$定义为$Q = [Zn^{2+}]/[Cu^{2+}]$,方括号表示相应离子的物质的量浓度。由于两个电池具有相同的$Zn^{2+}$和$Cu^{2+}$离子浓度,因此$Q$值相同,导致两个电池的电压相同。

然而,电池能够提供的电荷量取决于存在的电活性物种(金属离子和电极)的数量。电池$Y$在溶液中具有更高的金属离子浓度和更多的金属电极,因此在耗尽之前可以运行更长时间,尽管电压与电池$X$相同。

sample_understanding_summary.rationale: The task type is clear: compare the voltage and battery life of batteries X and Y. The answer format is also understandable. However, the provided image only shows a generic Zn/Pb cell diagram and does not include the referenced data below the diagram that should specify the solution concentrations and electrode masses for X and Y. Because that required visual context is missing, the image-text pair is not understandable enough for reliable annotation.

reject_records.reject_reason_detail: The correct option contains two atomic comparisons: one for voltage and one for battery life, so the question is split into two open-ended subquestions.
